developer.label: Developer
developer.description: The human using the extension. Owns the codebase being diagrammed, refines the model in conversation with the AI, and drags / clicks the rendered diagram to navigate and pin layout.

ai_agent.label: AI Agent
ai_agent.description: An LLM-driven coding agent (e.g. Claude in the editor). Reads BOOTSTRAP.md to learn the model schema and authoring rules, explores the developer's codebase, and writes model.json — iteratively, while the developer watches the diagram refresh.

bootstrap_spec.label: BOOTSTRAP.md
bootstrap_spec.description: The authoring guide the AI follows: data model, granularity rules, conversation/output protocol, and what will fail to render. The thing that turns 'go diagram this repo' into a valid model.json.

target_codebase.label: Target Codebase
target_codebase.description: The project being diagrammed — README, manifests, source files. The AI explores it to decide what the architectural boxes are. (In this meta diagram, the target codebase IS the project-viewer source.)

vscode.label: VS Code
vscode.description: The editor that hosts the extension and is what the developer is actually looking at. Provides the command palette, status bar, webview, and filesystem APIs the extension consumes.

model_json.label: .viewer/model.json
model_json.description: The source of truth. The diagram is purely derived from this file — change the JSON and the diagram refreshes live. Hand-authored, AI-authored, or some mix of both.

elk.label: ELK
elk.description: Eclipse Layout Kernel (elkjs). Given a graph of nodes and edges, returns deterministic positions. Used by the renderer for any node the developer hasn't pinned via drag.

vscode_extension.label: VS Code Extension
vscode_extension.description: The VS Code-side of Project Viewer. Splits across two processes — a Node.js extension host and a Chromium webview iframe — that communicate via postMessage. Lives at src/vscode_extension/ + src/diagnostics/.

diagram_engine.label: Diagram Engine
diagram_engine.description: Pure logic. Defines the Diagram type, reads/writes model.json on disk, renders Diagram → SVG via ELK. No VS Code dependency — could in principle be lifted out and used in a CLI or web app. Lives at src/diagram/.

extension_host.label: Extension Host (Node.js)
extension_host.description: Activate/deactivate, status bar, panel + file watcher, drag-pin persistence, click-to-jump anchor resolution. Imports the diagram engine to render. Lives at src/vscode_extension/ (everything except webview.ts).

webview.label: Webview (Chromium iframe)
webview.description: Renders SVG, handles drag, click, pan/zoom, the legend overlay, the reset modal. Posts user events back to the host via postMessage. Lives at src/vscode_extension/webview.ts.

diagnostics.label: Diagnostics
diagnostics.description: Cross-cutting event-tracing infrastructure. Pure (no VS Code dependency) — recorder + sinks. Both the extension host and the webview emit events; webview events bridge over postMessage into the host's recorder, which writes the merged stream to .viewer/in-progress/drag.log. __DEBUG__-gated; dead-stripped in production builds. Could in principle attach to other consumers (CLI, tests). Lives at src/diagnostics/.

metadata.what: data model, granularity rules, conversation protocol, anti-patterns

metadata.how: README, manifests, then ~10–15 entry-point source files

metadata.cadence: early and often — writes after first 2–3 components, then keeps refining

metadata.how: via conversation with the agent, or by editing JSON directly

metadata.when: developer clicks a component with a file/function/symbol anchor

_notes: Higher-level / system view: actors (developer, AI agent), the spec the agent follows (BOOTSTRAP.md), the codebase the agent reads, and the extension+model+ELK runtime. Extension internals (panel/renderer/watcher/storage/webview) are collapsed into one 'project_viewer' box — drop in if you want them broken out again, or nest them as children with parent='project_viewer'.
